data.title: Former US Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen: Still believes the Fed may cut interest rates this year.
data.description: Former US Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen said she still believes a US interest rate cut is possible later this year, although oil price volatility triggered by the Iran war has cast a shadow over the outlook. Yellen stated, "This is actually a broad supply shock, spreading from gasoline prices to liquefied natural gas, fertilizers, food, shipping costs, and semiconductors." Yellen said that while she doesn't rule out the possibility of raising interest rates, stable long-term inflation expectations suggest that this is unlikely at present. "I think my guess is that there might be an interest rate cut by the end of this year. I think that's entirely possible, and the most likely scenario. However, many things can happen." (Jinshi)

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Gold Jewelry Prices Rise in China
data.description: On April 15, Jin10 reported that gold jewelry prices in China have increased compared to the previous day. According to Jin10, Laopu Gold's pure gold jewelry is priced at 1,478 yuan per gram, marking a 25 yuan increase from yesterday. Chow Sang Sang's pure gold jewelry (selling price) is listed at 1,481 yuan per gram, up by 26 yuan from the previous day.

data.title: Ethereum Follow Protocol Integrates with Etherscan for Enhanced User Experience
data.description: Ethereum Follow Protocol (EFP) has successfully integrated with the blockchain explorer Etherscan, enhancing user interaction capabilities. According to Foresight News, brantly.eth, a core contributor to EFP, announced that users can now view ENS domain names, avatars, and on-chain EFP follower and following counts directly from the 'Cards' tab on each account page within Etherscan. This integration aims to streamline access to social data on the Ethereum blockchain, providing users with a more comprehensive view of their digital identity and social connections.

data.title: U.S. Interest Rate Cut Possible Later This Year, Says Former Treasury Secretary Yellen
data.description:
Former U.S. Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen has expressed that a potential interest rate cut in the United States later this year remains plausible. According to Jin10, Yellen highlighted that the oil price volatility triggered by the ongoing conflict in Iran has cast uncertainty over the economic outlook. She described the situation as a widespread supply shock affecting various sectors, including gasoline prices, liquefied natural gas, fertilizers, food, shipping costs, and semiconductors.

Yellen acknowledged the possibility of needing to raise interest rates but noted that stable long-term inflation expectations suggest such a scenario is unlikely at present. "I think my guess is that there might be rate cuts by the end of this year. I believe this is entirely possible and the most likely scenario. However, many things could happen," she stated.

data.title: Santiment Identifies Bullish Signal as Small Wallets Sell ETH
data.description: According to NS3.AI, Santiment has reported that wallets holding less than 0.01 ETH have sold a total of 1,791 ETH, valued at $4.16 million, over the past two days. Santiment interprets this activity as a bullish signal for Ethereum (ETH). The investors behind these wallets perceive ETH's approximate 19% increase since March 29 as a potential bull trap, prompting their decision to sell.

data.title: Bitcoin's 24-hour trading volume peaked at $1.75 billion, and zero-fee trading for BTC/U will launch on April 17, 2026.
data.description: U, a Chinese-language cryptocurrency, stated on the X platform that U, listed on Binance for three months, achieved a peak 24-hour trading volume of $1.75 billion. On April 13th, BTC/U trading volume surpassed BTC/USDT, and it has now entered the top ten tokens in terms of total trading volume. Zero-fee BTC/U trading will officially launch on April 17th, 2026 at 8:00 AM (UTC+8).

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $53.0329 million yesterday, marking the fourth consecutive day of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $53.0329 million yesterday (April 14th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the Fidelity ETF (FETH), with a net inflow of $38.0593 million, bringing FETH's historical total net inflow to $2.271 billion. This was followed by the BlackRock ETF (ETHA), with a net inflow of $10.4902 million, bringing ETHA's historical total net inflow to $11.737 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs was $13.393 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 4.79%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $11.73 billion.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Deems Iran's Nuclear Armament Unacceptable
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump declared on April 15 that the prospect of Iran acquiring nuclear weapons is entirely unacceptable. According to NS3.AI, this statement underscores the ongoing tensions between the United States and Iran regarding nuclear proliferation. The U.S. administration has consistently expressed concerns over Iran's nuclear ambitions, emphasizing the need for stringent measures to prevent the development of nuclear arms in the region.

data.title: Stocks Surge Over 10% Following Iran Ceasefire
data.description: Stocks have experienced a significant rise of more than 10% since the announcement of a ceasefire in the Iran conflict last week. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting market reactions that suggest the war is perceived as concluded by investors. The ceasefire has brought a sense of relief to the markets, leading to a positive shift in stock performance. Analysts are observing the situation closely to assess the long-term implications of this development on global markets.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $411 million yesterday, with BlackRock's IBIT leading the way with a net inflow of $214 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $411 million yesterday (April 14th, Eastern Time). The BlackRock ETF IBIT saw the largest single-day net inflow, at $214 million, bringing its total historical net inflow to $63.975 billion. Following closely was the Ark Invest and 21Shares ETF ARKB, with a single-day net inflow of $113 million, bringing its total historical net inflow to $1.502 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs was $96.563 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of total Bitcoin market capitalization) of 6.51%, and a cumulative historical net inflow of $56.864 billion.

data.title: Trump: Iran possessing nuclear weapons is absolutely unacceptable
data.description: On April 15, US President Trump stated: Iran possessing nuclear weapons is absolutely unacceptable. (Jinshi)

data.title: New Address Deposits 6.75 Million USDC to HyperLiquid for Brent Oil Position
data.description: On April 15, a newly created address deposited 6.75 million USDC into HyperLiquid, according to BlockBeats. The address opened a long position in Brent Oil with a leverage of four times. Additionally, the address has a TWAP order set to further increase the position within the next six hours.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Discusses Potential Outcomes of Agreement
data.description: On April 15, U.S. President Donald Trump commented on the potential outcomes of a current situation. According to BlockBeats, Trump indicated that there are two possible scenarios, but reaching an agreement would be more beneficial for reconstruction.

data.title: BitMart contracts will list MINIMAXUSDT, LENOVOUSDT, AKESOUSDT, and CITICUSDT.
data.description: According to the official announcement, BitMart will launch perpetual contracts for MINIMAXUSDT, LENOVOUSDT, AKESOUSDT, and CITICUSDT on April 15, 2026 at 14:00 (UTC+8), supporting leverage up to 20x.

data.title: Polymarket Account Profits Surpass $1 Million After Hornets Victory
data.description: A Polymarket account has significantly increased its earnings following the Hornets' victory over the Heat in the NBA Play-In market. According to NS3.AI, the account gained approximately $212,300 from this event, pushing its total profits to over $1 million. This achievement highlights the potential for substantial gains in sports betting markets.

data.title: The US military claims it has completely cut off Iran's maritime import and export trade.
data.description: Late Tuesday local time, U.S. Central Command Commander Brad Cooper stated, "The blockade of Iranian ports has been fully implemented. In less than 36 hours since the blockade was implemented, the U.S. military has completely cut off Iran's maritime import and export trade." Cooper stated that it is estimated that 90% of Iran's economy relies on international maritime trade. (Jinshi)

data.title: Anthropic Surpasses OpenAI in Pre-IPO Valuation
data.description:
On April 15, data revealed that Anthropic's valuation in Hyperliquid's Pre-IPO market segment has surpassed that of OpenAI. According to BlockBeats, Anthropic is currently valued at $934 per share, equating to a total valuation of $934 billion, marking a 6% increase over the past 24 hours. In contrast, OpenAI's valuation stands at $824 per share, corresponding to an $824 billion valuation, with a 6.3% decrease in the same period.

Despite these valuation changes, the market is experiencing very low trading volumes. Anthropic's trading pair recorded a 24-hour trading volume of only $372,000, while OpenAI's trading pair saw a 24-hour trading volume of just $123,000.

data.title: STOCKS | China's A-Shares See Mixed Performance Amid Sector Rotation
data.description:
On April 15, China's A-shares experienced a mixed performance as the three major indices opened higher but saw varied movements throughout the morning session. According to Jin10, the ChiNext Index initially rose over 1%, reaching its highest level since June 2015, but later fell by 0.23% by midday. Meanwhile, the Shanghai Composite Index increased by 0.37%, and the Shenzhen Component Index decreased by 0.25%. The combined trading volume of the Shanghai and Shenzhen markets reached 1.58 trillion yuan, an increase of 84.4 billion yuan from the previous trading day. Over 2,500 stocks saw gains across the market.

Sector rotation accelerated, with the innovative drug sector remaining active. Stocks such as Borui Pharmaceutical, Ruikang Pharmaceutical, Hisun Pharmaceutical, and Jinling Pharmaceutical hit their daily limit. The superconducting concept saw a rise and fall, with Jianxin Superconducting reaching a 20% limit up, followed by gains in Western Materials, Jingyuan Environmental Protection, and Jinbei Electric. The MicroLED sector also saw gains, with Zhiyun Co. and Woge Optoelectronics hitting their daily limit. Other sectors, including CPO, foldable screens, commercial aerospace, and ultra-high voltage, were among the top gainers. Conversely, chemical stocks declined, with Furui Co. nearing its limit down. Gas stocks, silicon energy, and real estate sectors experienced fluctuations and declines.

data.title: The Hornets defeated the Heat in the NBA play-in tournament, resulting in a profit of over $1 million. The account's profit for the single game was approximately $210,000.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, the outcome of the "NBA Play-In Game Hornets vs. Heat" prediction event on Polymarket has been confirmed: the Hornets won the game, with a total trading volume of approximately $401,300. Accounts that bought positions in the Hornets' winning prediction and have already profited over $1 million saw a profit of approximately $212,300 in this single game. Odaily Seer previously reported that this account had purchased approximately $500,000 worth of Hornets winning positions, with an average opening price of 68.8¢. Odaily Seer continues to monitor the prediction market, seeing changes before prices are priced in.

data.title: Singapore Government Bonds Outperform US Treasuries Amid Rising Haven Demand
data.description: Singapore government bonds are experiencing a significant outperformance over US Treasuries, reaching levels not seen since 2007. Bloomberg posted on X that this trend is driven by a decline in local interbank borrowing costs, indicating a surge in demand for safe-haven assets. The shift reflects investor sentiment favoring Singapore's stable economic environment amid global uncertainties. Analysts suggest that the robust performance of Singapore bonds could continue as geopolitical tensions and economic challenges persist globally.
